results.title: The Story of the letter post
results.creator_credit: David Morphet, Oswald Skilbeck and General Post Office
results.brief_description: this film recounts the gradual development of the British mail service from the fifteenth century, reconstructed with period costumes, museum pieces, and old stamps. Early use of messengers and Henry VIII’s service expanded for public use by Charles I; 1660 Act of Parliament; Ralph Allan’s reforms; the Industrial Revolution; improved roads and the mail coach; Rowland Hill’s penny post and modern mechanical methods.

results.title: The Story of sugar
results.brief_description: This classroom film sets out to describe how the white sugar we use every day at mealtimes reaches us in the form we know it best - as white crystals. Although the film deals exclusively with cane sugar, and does not mention the production of sugar from sugar-beet, the films gives a fairly clear picture of the production of sugar in the canefields of northern Australia, and the refining processes carried out in the cities further south.

results.title: The Story of Rosy Dock [DVD]
results.brief_description: The story of an elderly woman who ventures into the central Australian desert to set up her home. In her attempt to recreate the beauty of the home she has left, she does not appreciate the beauty that is already there. Written and designed by author and artist Jeannie Baker, and based on her book of the same name, this animated film uses a unique collage of natural materials. It delivers a powerful message about the seemingly harmless practice of importing foreign flora, and how the Earth’s delicately balanced environment is continually being threatened by human interference. Narrated by Michael Craig. Teachers notes are available as a separate loan item with the VHS [306347].

results.title: The Story of prehistoric man
results.brief_description:
Using artefacts, cave paintings and skeletal remains this film describes the life, appearance, habitat and achievements of prehistoric man including the Old and New Stone Ages.
Suitable for upper primary and lower secondary levels.

results.title: The Story of Port Augusta
results.brief_description: A description of the town of Port Augusta in South Australia, giving something of its history, its industries, transport and way of life. Narrated by Roger Climpson.

results.title: The Story of Okra John = L'Histoire d'Okra John
results.creator_credit: Magnus Einarsson, Pierre Stevens, Roland Pirker and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: Professional storyteller Joan Bodger entertains an audience with a traditional Black folk tale about the tribulations of a slave named Okra John, and how he came to Canada via the “underground railroad.”

results.title: The Story of molecules
results.creator_credit: Education Media Australia and Iwanami Productions
results.brief_description: This film enables students to examine some simple evidence for the existence of molecules. Reference to the beliefs of Democritus and Plato are mentioned and also to the origin of the word “atom”. It gives an opportunity to calculate the size of a molecule and some models of common molecules are shown. The film includes actual photographs of molecules produced by the modern electron microscope.
